content.problemStatement:
Given an M×NM×NM×N matrix of integers, find the rectangle (submatrix) that has the maximum sum of its elements. The rectangle can be of any size from 1×11×11×1 up to M×NM×NM×N.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains MMM and N,N,N, dimensions of matrix
Next MMM lines contain NNN space-separated integers each




Output Format

For each test case, print the maximum sum possible from any rectangle in the matrix.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers M and N, the number of rows and columns of the matrix. Then M lines follow, each containing N space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum possible sum of any rectangular sub-matrix.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The rectangle formed by rows 2-3 and columns 2-4 (0-based) gives the sum 29, which is the maximum possible.

Test case 1: all numbers are positive, so the whole matrix sum is 4.
Test case 2: all numbers are negative, the best we can do is pick the largest element -1.
All entries are negative; the maximum sum rectangle is the single element -1.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by using Kadane's algorithm to find the maximum sum of a subarray in a 1D array. We can apply this algorithm to each row of the matrix and then to each possible sub-matrix. The maximum sum of a sub-matrix can be found by iterating over all possible top and bottom rows and applying Kadane's algorithm to the sum of the elements in the rows.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * M^2 * N) per test case, O(T * M^2 * N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — for storing the temporary array.
